assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_029",
  "rule_id": "Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.97,
  "rationale": "The remediation only adds or updates the StopIdleSessionSec setting in /etc/systemd/logind.conf and restarts systemd-logind. This does not affect authentication credentials, bootloader, or filesystem layout and is a standard STIG hardening measure for session timeout.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "May terminate legitimate idle user sessions",
    "Incorrect syntax could prevent logind from parsing the file"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Backup /etc/systemd/logind.conf",
    "Add or update the [Login] section with StopIdleSessionSec=<desired_seconds>",
    "Run 'systemctl daemon-reload' and 'systemctl restart systemd-logind'",
    "Verify the setting with 'loginctl show-session $XDG_SESSION_ID -p IdleHint' or similar",
    "Ensure login functionality remains after change"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": true,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
assistant_message.reasoning:
We need to classify the finding: "Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity". This involves editing /etc/systemd/logind.conf to set StopIdleSessionSec=. This is a systemd login manager config, affects session timeout. Not risky, typical STIG. So safe_to_remediate. Confidence high, maybe 0.97.

Rationale: adjusting logind config does not lock out, only terminates idle sessions.

Risk_factors: potential to terminate legitimate idle sessions; minimal.

Safe_next_steps: backup logind.conf, edit or use authselect/ansible, run systemctl restart systemd-logind, verify.

Requires_reboot: No, just restart service.

Touches_authn_authz: Yes, deals with session management (auth). It is authn/authz touch.

Touches_networking: No.

Touches_filesystems: No.

Provide JSON.
